topics.title: Deep Tech
topics.executive_summary: Nuclear startup Deep Fission is pursuing a Nasdaq IPO seeking up to $1.66 billion valuation despite worsening financials and timeline slippage, raising questions about whether it's capitalizing on sector hype rather than actual progress.

stories.headline: Nuclear Startup Deep Fission's Confusing IPO Comeback Raises Eyebrows
stories.body: Deep Fission announced it's going public on Nasdaq seeking $157 million at a $1.66 billion valuation—just months after a previous 'public' listing that never actually traded. The company's deficit has grown to $88.1 million and it admits it could run out of money in 12 months without the IPO.
